title: Popover
description: Foldkit-native Base UI Popover primitive preserving model-owned open state, trigger/open/close facts, deterministic modal and placement attributes, popup ARIA relationships, command-facing focus and scroll-lock helpers, backdrop, arrow, and title/description wiring.

runtime.reason: Provides Html, Attribute, Command, Dom, and message helpers for the Foldkit-native primitive.
Provides Effect Schema, Option, Predicate, and Effect composition for serializable options and command helpers.

development.reason: Origin Base UI React Popover primitive is replaced by this Foldkit implementation.
Origin Base UI React Popover primitive is replaced by this Foldkit implementation.
React is origin fixture evidence only, not installable Foldkit source.

deviations.summary: Open state is a model-owned input and user interactions emit open-change facts instead of mutating internal component state.
deviations.rationale: Foldkit preserves unidirectional data flow. Consumers update their model and return command helpers in response to PopoverOpenChange payloads.

deviations.summary: Focus handoff and focus restoration are exposed as explicit Command helpers rather than hidden inside render helpers.
deviations.rationale: Popover side effects must stay at Foldkit's Command boundary. The installable view remains pure and deterministic.

deviations.summary: Placement is represented through deterministic side, align, offset, collision, positioner, and arrow attributes without performing DOM measurement or collision flipping.
deviations.rationale: Real collision handling depends on measured geometry and should be added through a shared measurement service, not an ad hoc render helper side effect.

deviations.summary: The modal surface is exposed with data and ARIA attributes, and modal=true locks scroll through commands, but pointer isolation and non-dialog trap-focus are not fully implemented.
deviations.rationale: Foldkit currently provides scroll lock, focus, and dialog-specific modal helpers, but this registry item does not have a shared non-dialog focus-trap or pointer-isolation command primitive. The 'trap-focus' mode is therefore command-facing and explicit rather than hidden inside render helpers.
